message:
docs(AGENTS): record redactor module + policy-denial audit rows

/reality-check-for-project pass 3 scoped to AGENTS.md. Module map at
lines 290-303 was missing two rows that became real after recent
refactors:

1. ft-siwlu (Redactor extraction, landed via f540a5d5 → today's
   uncommitted work → redactor.rs pub struct): `Redactor` now lives in
   `crates/frankenterm-core/src/redactor.rs`; policy.rs carries a
   `pub use crate::redactor::Redactor` compat shim so existing imports
   still work. Module map previously only listed policy.rs and didn't
   name the redactor module.

2. ft-h90rh / ft-rsqap / ft-mw1zb (policy-denial audit stream): new
   policy_denied_audit SQLite table (schema v24, migrated in the h90rh
   ship) + PolicyDeniedAuditRecord + sync/async persist paths. Module
   map previously had no row naming the dedicated audit stream;
   operators reading the map had no entry point for "where do denied
   MCP mutations land."

Other AGENTS.md claims spot-checked and still green:
- Rust 2024 edition (AGENTS.md:108 ↔ Cargo.toml:89) ✓
- #![forbid(unsafe_code)] via [workspace.lints.rust] (AGENTS.md:111 ↔
  Cargo.toml:96 `unsafe_code = "forbid"`) ✓
- Release profile (AGENTS.md:140-147 ↔ Cargo.toml:548-552 line-for-line
  per the ft-zo3cr audit earlier) ✓
- asupersync as the intended runtime (AGENTS.md:113-118) — still holds,
  runtime_compat remains the audited seam
- Module locations for runtime / runtime_compat / ingest / storage /
  patterns / events / workflows / policy / wezterm — all still correct

No other claims found drifted; AGENTS.md doesn't track feature-list or
module-count numbers (those live in README and are validated elsewhere).

Agent: RusticMeadow

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Use the mission/tx golden-capture pattern, seed storage directly, and pin success plus invalid-args envelopes for wa.reserve, wa.reservations, and wa.release without touching mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:36:28.848013Z","created_by":"LilacBay","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:19:58.028447Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:19:58.028214Z","close_reason":"Shipped in d4e41dca","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-4o5mb.3","depends_on_id":"ft-4o5mb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:36:28.848013Z","created_by":"LilacBay","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":3622,"issue_id":"ft-4o5mb.3","author":"jemanuel","text":"Switching off this bead immediately: LilacBay already holds an exclusive reservation on crates/frankenterm-core/tests/wa_reservations_mcp_conformance.rs, so this would collide with pane 4. Moving to fallback bead wa-nu4.3.9.19 instead of contending on the same conformance file.","created_at":"2026-04-23T19:14:20Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-4o5mb.4","title":"[conformance] wa.tx_* lacks TOON envelope parity and boundary invalid-params coverage","description":"`crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mcp_conformance_mission_tx.rs` already freezes JSON success/error goldens for `wa.tx_plan`, `wa.tx_show`, `wa.tx_run`, and `wa.tx_rollback`, but it does not exercise `format: "toon"` envelopes and it does not pin fastmcp boundary invalid-params behavior for wrong-typed / missing required fields.

Gap: tx MCP callers can regress TOON semantics or input-boundary contracts without any conformance failure, even though the schemas advertise json/toon negotiation and typed inputs in `tests/fixtures/mcp_manifest.json`.

Fix: add conformance captures that compare TOON vs JSON semantic envelopes and freeze one boundary invalid-params response per tool.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:20:25.357797Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:59:48.933217Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T20:59:48.933049Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 0f744567: add wa.tx TOON conformance harness (verify partial)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-4o5mb.4","depends_on_id":"ft-4o5mb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:20:25.357797Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-4o5mb.5","title":"[conformance] wa.mission_* lacks TOON envelope parity and boundary invalid-params coverage","description":"`crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mcp_conformance_mission_tx.rs` already freezes JSON success/error goldens for `wa.mission_state`, `wa.mission_explain`, `wa.mission_pause`, `wa.mission_resume`, and `wa.mission_abort`, but it does not exercise `format: "toon"` envelopes and it does not pin fastmcp boundary invalid-params behavior for wrong-typed / missing required fields.

Gap: mission MCP callers can regress TOON semantics or input-boundary contracts without any conformance failure, even though the tool schemas advertise json/toon negotiation and typed inputs in `tests/fixtures/mcp_manifest.json`.

Fix: add per-tool conformance capture that (a) asserts TOON envelope parity against the JSON success path and (b) records the framework invalid-params response for one representative wrong-typed required field per tool.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:20:55.455807Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:47:44.068592Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T20:47:13.917290Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 62744f55: add wa.mission TOON conformance harness (verify partial)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-4o5mb.5","depends_on_id":"ft-4o5mb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:20:55.455807Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"ft-4o5mb.6","title":"[conformance] wa.events_annotate / wa.events_triage / wa.events_label lack schema+golden roundtrip","description":"Manifest coverage audit shows the event mutator trio still lacks per-tool MCP conformance goldens. Existing conformance files cover wa.events, wa.reserve/reservations/release, wa.state, wa.search/get_text/send/wait_for, wa.mission_*, and wa.tx_*; no integration harness currently freezes wa.events_annotate, wa.events_triage, or wa.events_label success envelopes plus framework invalid-params behavior against tests/fixtures/mcp_manifest.json. Fix: add one focused integration test file that seeds a real event row, freezes each tool's live input schema, captures canonical success envelopes, and records one representative boundary-invalid failure per tool into golden_robot_envelope/wa_event_mutations_conformance.json.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-23T21:43:21.762917Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T21:43:21.762917Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","mcp","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-4o5mb.6","depends_on_id":"ft-4o5mb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-23T21:43:21.762917Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-4qom2","title":"[SEV] tmux cmd_queue cap only enforced at pop site; unbounded growth when state != Idle","description":"frankenterm/mux/src/tmux.rs TmuxDomainState.cmd_queue has CMD_QUEUE_MAX_DEPTH=50_000 enforced only inside send_next_command at line 289 via cmd_queue.drain(..excess). send_next_command early-returns at line 286-288 when state != Idle, so the drain-oldest logic never runs if tmux is stuck in State::WaitingForResponse.

Four push sites append to cmd_queue with NO cap check:
- line 196: Event::LayoutChange -> ListAllPanes
- line 223: Event::SessionChanged -> ListCommands
- line 234: Event::WindowAdd -> ListAllWindows
- line 375: split_pane (user split) -> SplitPane

Concrete interleaving: (1) tmux sends a command, enters State::WaitingForResponse; (2) tmux server is slow/hung (real-world scenario: remote tmux on lagging SSH link, tmux server overloaded, Event::Guarded response delayed); (3) GUI user resizes window repeatedly -> stream of Event::LayoutChange -> each push_back at line 196 runs without cap check; (4) send_next_command early-returns because state != Idle. Queue grows unbounded -> memory leak, eventual OOM.

The CMD_QUEUE_MAX_DEPTH comment at line 32-33 explicitly advertises 'prevent unbounded memory growth during event storms' as a safety invariant. That invariant is currently only upheld at the pop site, where it can't run under the exact condition (stuck tmux) that event storms exploit.

Fix: extract the cap-enforcement logic into a push_command_capped helper that applies the drain-oldest + warn/error logging at every push site. The helper runs under the cmd_queue lock (just acquired by the caller), so it's atomic with the push and has no additional locking cost.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T00:17:26.601099Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T00:21:03.160715Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T00:21:03.158111Z","close_reason":"Completed: added push_command_capped() helper and routed all 4 production push sites (LayoutChange/SessionChanged/WindowAdd/split_pane) through it. The hard cap is now enforced at push time, not only at pop time — closing the unbounded-growth window when tmux is stuck in State::WaitingForResponse. cargo check passes clean. Commit 1055cffc.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-4r2ib","title":"[mission][dispatch] MissionDispatcher reports accepted dispatch without calling WorkflowRunner::handle_detection","description":"In `crates/frankenterm-core/src/mission_dispatch.rs:200-226`, `dispatch_single()` builds a synthetic detection, then only calls `runner.find_matching_workflow(&detection)` and fabricates `accepted: true` plus a synthetic `execution_id` when a workflow exists. It never invokes `WorkflowRunner::handle_detection()`, so no lock acquisition, no concurrency-limit enforcement, no storage row, and no real workflow start happen.

Impact: callers receive a success-shaped `DispatchResult` that claims work was accepted even though the runner was never asked to start anything.

Fix direction: bridge the sync mission loop to the async runner start path with a bounded runtime, map the actual `WorkflowStartResult` into `DispatchResult`, and only emit started metadata from a real start result.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:09:03.405716Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:16:34.545322Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T20:16:34.544924Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 0761bb33: mission dispatch now calls WorkflowRunner::handle_detection and returns real start metadata","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-4z7vh","title":"RobotCommands::Workflow builds PolicyEngine without require_prompt_active/command_gate/rules — bypasses operator safety config","description":"AUTH BYPASS (robot mode mutations, Severity: Medium).

crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs has 10 PolicyEngine::new call sites. 8 of them wire require_prompt_active from config + .with_command_gate_config(config.safety.command_gate.clone()) + .with_policy_rules(config.safety.rules.clone()). Two do NOT, both under RobotCommands::Workflow:

Site 1 (main.rs:19831, RobotCommands::Workflow::Run):

  let policy_engine = PolicyEngine::new(
      config.safety.rate_limit_per_pane,
      config.safety.rate_limit_global,
      false, // Don't require prompt active for robot mode
  )
  .with_tuning(&config.tuning);

No command_gate, no policy_rules, and require_prompt_active hardcoded false. This engine is fed into CxPolicyInjector → WorkflowRunner; any workflow step that calls send_text (e.g., handle_compaction sending '/compact', handle_usage_limit sending '/refresh') bypasses:
  - the operator's safety.require_prompt_active=true policy,
  - every [[safety.rules]] custom gate,
  - the [safety.command_gate] allow/deny list.

Site 2 (main.rs:20511, RobotCommands::Workflow::Abort): identical construction. abort_execution_with_cx runs compensation steps through the same injector; any send_text in a compensation path bypasses the same three surfaces.

Compare the correctly-gated paths:
  - RobotCommands::Send at main.rs:17652 uses config.safety.require_prompt_active AND .with_command_gate_config + .with_policy_rules.
  - Non-robot workflow invocations at main.rs:21361, 21533, 25051, 27347 all wire the full policy surface.

NOT a duplicate of ft-48ah6 (read-only policy): this bead is specifically about the write/mutation surface (workflow's send_text / command-bearing steps). ft-48ah6 covered the read/search side.

Fix: replace the hardcoded 'false' with config.safety.require_prompt_active at both sites, and chain .with_command_gate_config(config.safety.command_gate.clone()) + .with_policy_rules(config.safety.rules.clone()) before passing the engine to PolicyGatedInjector. Rate-limit args are already correct.

Regression test: a send inside a robot-invoked workflow must be DENIED when config.safety.require_prompt_active=true AND no prompt is active, matching the direct-send gate behavior. Currently it is allowed.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane5","created_at":"2026-04-23T06:46:59.925741Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T07:19:54.084786Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T07:19:54.084480Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 834b59f2. Both RobotCommands::Workflow::Run (main.rs:19831) and ::Abort (main.rs:20520) now build PolicyEngine with config.safety.require_prompt_active and chain .with_command_gate_config + .with_policy_rules — matching the construction at RobotCommands::Send (main.rs:17652) and non-robot workflow sites (21361/21533/25051/27347). Regression test robot_workflow_policy_engine_denies_send_when_prompt_required_ft_4z7vh asserts SendText is denied when require_prompt_active=true + command_running. Test binary compile is currently blocked by pre-existing concurrent-agent breakage (E0603 macro select private, E0063 missing defer_fts_triggers field) unrelated to this fix.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
@@ -166,7 +167,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2","title":"[asupersync][vendored] Mux client/pool/runtime migration completion","description":"Drive completion of DirectMuxClient/MuxPool/runtime-adjacent migrations and align semantics with core doctrine.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Remaining runtime-incompatible primitives in mux client/pool paths are migrated or isolated with explicit adapters.\n2. Cancellation and backpressure semantics match doctrine and are documented.\n3. Unit tests cover mux path behavior under cancellation/retry/load.\n4. Integration tests validate core<->mux interactions after migration.\n5. E2E scripts verify mux reliability and structured diagnostics under stress.","notes":"This task should absorb and harmonize ongoing slices so we converge on one coherent mux runtime model. Heavy compile/test/benchmark/soak workloads for this bead must run via rch exec -- <command>, with reproducible command records and artifact paths captured in completion evidence. Include explicit rollback/fallback/recovery validation criteria with pass/fail outcomes for user-facing workflows. Require deterministic fixtures/seeds and replayable test evidence so failures are reproducible across environments. All diagnostics/logging/artifact outputs in this bead must comply with secret-safe redaction and privacy policy gates defined in ft-e34d9.10.7.5, with evidence captured in validation artifacts.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:26:19.410169Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T05:44:57.229937Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T05:44:57.229640Z","close_reason":"Sole child .2 closed - epic rollup complete","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","e2e-tests","integration-tests","logging","mux","quality-gate","sequencing","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact","vendored"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2","depends_on_id":"ft-2h5wv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2","depends_on_id":"ft-p48pw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2449,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2","author":"SilverBluff","text":"SilverBluff starting a bounded 2026-03-22 support slice under ft-e34d9.10.5.2. Scope is test-only: use the existing clean integration surface in crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mux_migration_completion.rs to add any missing timeout-cascade and recovery-path assertions without touching the active mux_client.rs implementation work. Goal is to strengthen the mux migration verification lane while staying off adjacent in-flight source files.","created_at":"2026-03-22T03:17:19Z"},{"id":2450,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2","author":"SilverBluff","text":"Progress 2026-03-22 (SilverBluff): landed a bounded test-only support slice in crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mux_migration_completion.rs. Added two missing assertions on the clean integration surface without touching active mux_client.rs work: (1) pool_timeout_cascade_then_recovery_restores_capacity, which proves a timed-out nested acquire under capacity pressure does not leak permits or issue spurious mux reads and that the next healthy retry succeeds after release; (2) simulated_network_recovery_after_transient_fault_delivers_new_data, which proves a transient hostile-network fault can be followed by a healthy retry that delivers new payload bytes on the same underlying mock transport. Local hygiene checks are green: rustfmt --edition 2024 --check crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mux_migration_completion.rs ✅ and git diff --check on that file ✅. Targeted remote validation is in flight via rch on vmi1153651 using: env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target/rch-silverbluff-ft-e34d9-10-5-2 CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test mux_migration_completion --features asupersync-runtime,vendored recovery -- --test-threads=1. Current remote state: real compilation is progressing (not in immediate source-error or stale state), but rch emitted the existing dependency-topology fail-open warning for sibling path deps ([RCH-E327], primary-root-only sync).,workdir:","created_at":"2026-03-22T03:26:55Z"},{"id":2451,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2","author":"SilverBluff","text":"Follow-up 2026-03-22 (SilverBluff): I cancelled rch build 29759189920253462 after ~9 minutes because it was still in a healthy first-compile dependency walk on vmi1153651 and I was no longer waiting on the session. Important distinction: no immediate source-level compile failure surfaced before cancellation; the run had progressed through substantial dependency compilation after the existing [RCH-E327] dependency-topology fail-open warning. This slice should be treated as code-complete but still awaiting a resumed targeted rch validation pass if fresh remote evidence is needed.","created_at":"2026-03-22T03:28:18Z"},{"id":2544,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2","author":"JadeBeaver","text":"JadeBeaver bounded support slice 2026-03-27 under ft-e34d9.10.5.2: explicit-Cx cancellation was being ignored/misclassified at the runtime_compat semaphore + generic Pool boundary, which let vendored mux-pool explicit-Cx paths surface timeout semantics or proceed instead of failing as cancellation. Landed fix in runtime_compat.rs, pool.rs, vendored/mux_pool.rs, and a small downstream guard in wezterm.rs so PoolError::Cancelled does not trip the vendored circuit breaker. Added focused regressions for pre-cancelled pool/mux-pool operations and mid-wait permit cancellation, plus proptest coverage for the new PoolError variant. Local rustfmt --check and git diff --check are green. Remote evidence is in progress via valid offloaded runs 58601 and 77601; a separate run 66978 failed the remote dependency preflight with RCH-E324 and fell open local, so it was rejected and not used as validation evidence.","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:24:36Z"},{"id":2612,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2","author":"SunnyRaven","text":"Support slice ft-e34d9.10.5.2.2 completed on the clean integration harness only: crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mux_migration_completion.rs. Added pool_cancelled_cx_under_contention_then_recovery_restores_capacity, which proves a cancelled nested acquire while the only pool permit is held surfaces cancellation, preserves zero available permits, does not increment total_acquired, does not issue an extra mock mux request, and still allows the next healthy release/retry path to restore balance. Remote validation passed via rch on worker vmi1153651 with env TMPDIR=/tmp CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target/rch-ft-e34d9-10-5-2-2 cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test mux_migration_completion --features asupersync-runtime,vendored pool_cancelled_cx_under_contention_then_recovery_restores_capacity -- --test-threads=1 => 1 passed / 0 failed. Agent Mail is intermittently returning transient DB errors again, so this parent comment is the durable coordination record for the completion.","created_at":"2026-03-30T00:32:05Z"},{"id":3574,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2","author":"jemanuel","text":"cod1 support slice 2026-04-19: landed main commit 6a6f4ec5 on the clean integration harness only. Added spawn_with_cx_cancelled_pool_acquire_fails_and_healthy_retry_recovers to crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mux_migration_completion.rs and wired matching structural/targeted coverage into tests/e2e/test_ft_e34d9_10_5_2_mux_migration_completion.sh. Local bash -n and git diff --check passed. A local cargo test lane was started with CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang CXX=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-cod1-target cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test mux_migration_completion --features asupersync-runtime,vendored spawn_with_cx_cancelled_pool_acquire_fails_and_healthy_retry_recovers -- --nocapture; it stayed in a real first-build dependency compile rather than surfacing a source failure before I moved on.","created_at":"2026-04-19T21:20:41Z"},{"id":3584,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2","author":"jemanuel","text":"cod1 support slice 2026-04-19: added spawn_with_cx_timeout_pool_acquire_fails_and_healthy_retry_recovers to crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mux_migration_completion.rs and wired structural + targeted validation coverage into tests/e2e/test_ft_e34d9_10_5_2_mux_migration_completion.sh. Local bash -n and git diff --check passed. A targeted local cargo test lane was started with CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang CXX=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-cod1-target cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test mux_migration_completion --features asupersync-runtime,vendored spawn_with_cx_timeout_pool_acquire_fails_and_healthy_retry_recovers -- --nocapture; it remained in the real compile/link walk without surfacing a new source error before I checkpointed the slice.","created_at":"2026-04-19T22:24:59Z"},{"id":3593,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2","author":"jemanuel","text":"Support slice 2026-04-21: landed c1fb747e fix(vendored/mux_client): bind explicit-cx write timeout detail. The current vendored mux client already carried the explicit-Cx cancellation classification change; this follow-up fixes the remaining write-timeout match arm so timeout_with_cx passes the actual timeout detail into classify_cx_timeout instead of referring to an unbound name. Remote validation via rch surfaced this exact compile error in mux_client.rs, then also surfaced unrelated pre-existing frankenterm-core breakage in runtime.rs and mcp_tools.rs that prevented a full green run. rustfmt on mux_client.rs is currently blocked by a pre-existing parse-invalid PaneOutputSubscription block already present at HEAD around line 1891, so only git diff --check was used for local hygiene on this one-line delta.","created_at":"2026-04-21T16:54:25Z"},{"id":3594,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2","author":"jemanuel","text":"Pre-existing blocker tracked in ft-8jbpj. The validation lane that surfaced c1fb747e also found HEAD-level source breakage in crates/frankenterm-core/src/wezterm.rs plus a separate parse-invalid PaneOutputSubscription block in crates/frankenterm-core/src/vendored/mux_client.rs; see ft-8jbpj for exact file:line evidence and the rch command that surfaced it.","created_at":"2026-04-21T16:59:26Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2.2","title":"[ft-e34d9.10.5.2][support] Strengthen cancellation-under-contention invariants in mux migration completion harness","description":"Bounded test-only support slice under ft-e34d9.10.5.2. Scope is limited to crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mux_migration_completion.rs. Strengthen the currently shallow cancellation coverage by adding a contention-focused invariant: a cancelled acquire while the only pool permit is held must fail cleanly without leaking capacity or causing spurious downstream mux activity, and the subsequent healthy release/retry path must restore a balanced pool. Validate with rch-only targeted cargo evidence.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SunnyRaven","created_at":"2026-03-30T00:11:39.606026Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-30T00:31:08.441229Z","closed_at":"2026-03-30T00:31:08.441066Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","cancellation","integration-tests","test-fix","vendored"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2.2","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-30T00:11:39.606026Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2605,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2.2","author":"SunnyRaven","text":"Started bounded support slice on crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mux_migration_completion.rs only. Plan: strengthen cancellation-under-contention coverage so a cancelled nested acquire while the only pool permit is held fails cleanly without leaking capacity or causing spurious mux activity, then prove the subsequent healthy release/retry path restores balance. File reservation is active via Agent Mail #6191; validation will be rch-only targeted cargo.","created_at":"2026-03-30T00:11:57Z"},{"id":2607,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2.2","author":"SunnyRaven","text":"Patch landed in crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mux_migration_completion.rs only. Added a new cancellation-under-contention regression that mirrors the existing timeout-cascade recovery shape but uses user cancellation and stronger invariants: blocked acquire must surface cancellation, preserve zero available permits while the primary checkout is held, avoid incrementing total_acquired, and avoid issuing extra mock mux requests before a healthy release/retry restores balance. Local rustfmt and git diff --check are green. Targeted remote validation is in flight via rch on worker vmi1153651: env TMPDIR=/tmp CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target/rch-ft-e34d9-10-5-2-2 cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test mux_migration_completion --features asupersync-runtime,vendored pool_cancelled_cx_under_contention_then_recovery_restores_capacity -- --test-threads=1","created_at":"2026-03-30T00:17:11Z"},{"id":2611,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2.2","author":"SunnyRaven","text":"Remote validation completed cleanly on worker vmi1153651. Result: 1 passed / 0 failed. This confirms the new regression in crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mux_migration_completion.rs correctly proves a cancelled nested acquire under contention does not leak permits, does not count as a successful checkout, does not issue an extra mux request, and still allows the following healthy release/retry path to restore a balanced pool.","created_at":"2026-03-30T00:30:29Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-e8brh","title":"[MEDIUM] cancel_races_child_creation test has weak race coverage; passes trivially via propagation","description":"In commit 6a16ae0a (fix(ft-2xnop): add unix umask coverage ... +
cancellation concurrency tests), the test
\`cancel_races_child_creation_all_children_see_cancellation\` at
crates/frankenterm-core/src/cancellation.rs:2635-2676 claims to verify
the cancel+child race but does not reliably exercise the race window.

Structure:
\`\`\`rust
for _ in 0..20 {
    let parent = CancellationToken::new(...);
    let n_creators = 8;
    let barrier = Arc::new(Barrier::new(n_creators + 1));

    let handles: Vec<_> = (0..n_creators).map(|i| {
        let parent = parent.clone();
        let barrier = Arc::clone(&barrier);
        thread::spawn(move || {
            barrier.wait();
            parent.child(ScopeId(format!(\"c_{i}\")))
        })
    }).collect();

    barrier.wait();
    parent.cancel(ShutdownReason::UserRequested);
    // ... assert every child is cancelled
}
\`\`\`

Problems:

1. The barrier synchronizes the START of each thread's work but not
   the duration of \`parent.child(...)\`. On a machine with >=8 cores,
   the 8 creator threads typically finish \`parent.child(...)\` BEFORE
   the main thread reaches \`parent.cancel(...)\`. In that ordering, all
   children are pre-existing when cancel propagates — a trivial case
   covered by other tests in the file.

2. 20 sequential rounds with no scheduler perturbation (no \`yield_now\`,
   no sleep, no thread count above core count) rarely hit the interesting
   interleaving where cancel runs mid-child-creation.

3. The assertion passes even if \`parent.child()\` has a bug where it
   reads the \"parent cancelled\" flag AFTER registration but BEFORE
   propagation reaches it — because the test never creates that window.

Impact: false confidence in race-safe \`CancellationToken::child()\`
under concurrent cancel. A latent bug where a newly-created child
escapes both propagation (not-yet-registered) AND post-cancel detection
(child() reads stale flag) would not be caught.

Fix direction:
- Spawn more creators than CPU cores (e.g., 64) so some are preempted
  mid-call.
- Insert \`thread::yield_now()\` or a short sleep inside the creator
  body before calling \`parent.child()\` to widen the race window.
- Alternatively, use an inner barrier that creators wait on AFTER
  they've decided to create the child but BEFORE registration, so the
  main thread can force the interleaving.
- Consider using loom or shuttle for deterministic interleaving
  coverage instead of probabilistic races.

Severity: medium — test quality issue, not a proven correctness bug
in cancellation.rs. Filing because the test claims coverage it does
not deliver.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T07:25:11.821489Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T07:48:54.626483Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T07:48:54.626237Z","close_reason":"Hardened in 7ac677a6: creator count 8→64 (forces preemption on any host), per-thread yield_now + modulo 50µs sleep widens race, rounds 20→64, each creator samples is_cancelled() pre-child() and the round-level outcomes are checked so the test asserts the cancel-first ordering was actually exercised at least once — if it isn't, the test fails with a diagnostic pointing at loom/shuttle for deterministic coverage. Build clean; local run blocked by unrelated macOS dyld bottleneck under load.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cancellation","concurrency","review","test-gap","testing"]}

 {"id":"ft-eqtcq","title":"aegis_entropy_anomaly::EProcess::update propagates NaN e_value; permanently disables rejection","description":"EProcess::update calls likelihood_ratio(h, baseline_mean, baseline_std, collapse_mean, collapse_std). If any f64 input is NaN, log_h0 and log_h1 both become NaN, log_lr = NaN - NaN = NaN, log_lr.exp() = NaN, and clamp(1e-6, 1e6) passes NaN through because f64::clamp does not reject NaN. self.e_value *= NaN = NaN. The final clamp(1e-100, max_e) at update:226 also passes NaN through. Future is_rejected() returns (NaN >= threshold) = false, silently disabling anomaly detection for the lifetime of this EProcess. Matches ft-b4l62 / ft-yskcu / ft-c17iw / ft-n4fdx pattern — fail-closed on non-finite inputs at the update() boundary.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T18:03:02.334443Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T18:05:25.492018Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T18:05:25.491748Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-xbnl0.5.7","title":"Polish first-run setup, environment validation, and recovery entrypoints for operators","description":"# Goal
Make the project pleasant and trustworthy from the first real operator interaction: initial setup, environment validation, first successful control-plane run, and entry into recovery workflows.

This task exists because `ft-xbnl0.5.4` proves fresh-install and recovery scenarios, but the plan did not yet contain a dedicated implementation bead that improves those entrypoints themselves. A polished finish line should not merely detect that setup and recovery are rough; it should explicitly make them operator-friendly.","design":"# Design
1. Improve the first-run path so operators can get from clone or install to a validated, ready-to-use FrankenTerm workspace without reading source code or guessing about prerequisites.
2. Make environment validation and doctor output actionable, including missing dependency diagnosis, configuration bootstrap issues, restore-state problems, and recovery entrypoints.
3. Ensure the first-run and recovery surfaces point naturally into the new diagnostic, playbook, and acceptance tooling instead of acting like separate subsystems.
4. Back the flow with comprehensive unit coverage for setup or validation logic plus deterministic E2E scenarios that exercise clean-start, broken-environment, and recovery-entry cases with detailed logs and stored artifacts.
5. Keep the result honest: if some setup or recovery path is intentionally manual, document it clearly and make the product surface say so directly.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Operators can move from clean environment to validated first successful run using a documented and product-supported path rather than tribal knowledge.
2. Missing prerequisites, bad config, restore-state issues, and other common startup failures produce actionable doctor or status output with clear next steps.
3. Recovery entrypoints route operators toward the standard diagnostic and playbook surfaces rather than leaving them in ad hoc failure states.
4. Comprehensive unit tests cover setup-state detection, degraded-environment handling, and operator-facing error classification for the touched surfaces.
5. At least one deterministic E2E scenario exercises clean setup, one exercises a broken-environment or bad-config path, and one exercises recovery entry, all with detailed timestamped logging, artifact capture, and exact verification commands recorded.","notes":"This bead makes fresh-install and recovery usability an explicit implementation concern rather than something only checked at the very end by acceptance scenarios.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-03T20:08:11.049428Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-19T17:15:33.968982Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T17:15:33.968636Z","close_reason":"operator guidance landed; artifact docs/ft-xbnl0-5-7-completion-evidence.md","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["doctor","e2e-tests","goal-line","operator","recovery","setup","test-coverage","test-logging"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.7","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T20:08:12.565115Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.7","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T20:08:11.049428Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.7","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T20:08:11.711759Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.7","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T20:08:12.253037Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-xgs7n","title":"[testing-fuzzing] pin extract_delta overlap-existence invariant: previous.ends_with(current[..len-delta.len()])","description":"# Gap

proptest_ingest_extract_delta_fuzz.rs covers 5 MRs (crash-freedom,
identity, content-suffix, pure-append, determinism) + 2 inline
regression tests, but does NOT pin the primary semantic contract of
the algorithm: **when Content(delta) is returned, the non-delta head
of `current` must be a tail of `previous`**. Formally:

    extract_delta(previous, current, _) = Content(delta)
    ⇒ previous.ends_with(&current[..current.len() - delta.len()])

This is the invariant the sliding-window search ACTUALLY proves
(search_window[pos..] == current[..overlap_len], where
search_window = previous[search_start..], so previous ends with
current[..overlap_len]). Without it, the algorithm could return a
Content(delta) where the "non-delta prefix" is pure fabrication —
the existing MR3 suffix-check proves current ends with delta but
says nothing about the overlap being real.

Adding this test catches any regression in the overlap-candidate
acceptance logic (`search_window[pos..] == current[..overlap_len]`
on ingest.rs:1711) that accidentally accepts a non-matching window.

Fix: new proptest in proptest_ingest_extract_delta_fuzz.rs covering
every path (pure-append fast path + sliding-window search).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:04:40.819353Z","created_by":"pane3-ingest-mr","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:13:32.759851Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:13:32.759521Z","close_reason":"Landed in 5eb9f956 (fix(apc, backpressure, ingest): …, overlap-existence proptest) — extract_delta_content_overlap_exists_in_previous added to tests/proptest_ingest_extract_delta_fuzz.rs:207. Verified: 1 passed / 0 failed / 14 filtered out on --test proptest_ingest_extract_delta_fuzz (test run took 0.20s under 512 proptest cases). Pins the semantic contract that when Content(delta) returns, previous.ends_with(current[..current.len() - delta.len()]) — the 'overlap was real' claim that MR3's suffix-only check does not catch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ingest","proptest","testing"]}
 {"id":"ft-xicum","title":"session_restore delete_session returns false after cleaning orphaned checkpoint rows","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane8","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:50:56.388120Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:52:53.130618Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:52:53.130299Z","close_reason":"delete_session now returns true when it removed orphaned checkpoint or pane-state rows, not just when the parent mux_sessions row existed; added regression coverage for orphan cleanup.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"ft-xkpy1","title":"[ux][P3] Robot + MCP error-hint audit follow-up (beyond MCP_ERR_POLICY)","description":"## Context

/ux-audit sweep of robot + MCP surfaces. Primary finding closed as a trivial ship: all 7 MCP_ERR_POLICY deny sites in mcp_tools.rs (6 McpToolError::new sites + 1 McpEnvelope in the mcp_authorize_mcp_mutation gate helper) previously passed None as hint — leaving MCP clients with 'Read denied by policy' and nowhere to go. Now all seven route through a shared POLICY_DENY_HINT const naming two actionable recovery paths (config.safety.rules for operator review, policy_denied_audit table for decision context).

Secondary findings not in that ship, each worth a separate pass:

## 1. Robot error-code coverage drift vs README

Defined in crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:4549-4564:
  robot.invalid_args, robot.unknown_subcommand, robot.config_error,
  robot.fts_query_error, robot.approval_error, robot.pane_not_found,
  robot.reservation_conflict, robot.storage_error, robot.feature_not_available,
  robot.policy_denied, robot.timeout, robot.workflow_aborted,
  robot.workflow_error, robot.workflow_not_found, robot.not_implemented,
  robot.remote_text_unavailable

README.md §Error Handling (robot mode) lists robot.wezterm_not_running and robot.require_approval — neither is a defined ROBOT_ERR_* constant. Either the README is stale (likely) or those codes exist under a different name (possible). Audit the README table against the actual constants + per-subcommand emission paths, update whichever is drifted.

## 2. Robot-mode hint uniformity

Walk every  construction in main.rs. MCP_ERR_POLICY just got treated; robot.pane_not_found, robot.timeout, robot.feature_not_available, etc. may have similar 'no-hint' patterns. Check each, add actionable hints matching the /ux-audit test: 'say what to do, not just what went wrong.'

## 3. Format-flag claims (toon vs json)

README claims  saves '40-60% fewer tokens' for AI-to-AI use. Spot-verify: render a few representative envelopes in both formats, measure actual byte counts, confirm the claim holds within some band. If TOON is actually ~15% smaller on small envelopes, the marketing text should be tightened.

## 4. Success-response conciseness

Every MCP tool envelope carries mcp_version, version, ok, data, elapsed_ms, now — 6 top-level keys. For small data payloads (e.g. a 2-item events list) the envelope can be 3-4x the size of the actual data. Consider whether any of mcp_version / version / now are redundant for the client once an initialize handshake has happened; file a separate architectural bead if so.

## Acceptance Criteria per item

- Item 1: README drift closed (either codes renamed in docs, or missing constants added in main.rs), with an assertion test that every documented code exists as a const.
- Item 2: Every robot-mode error construction reviewed; at least one shared const (like POLICY_DENY_HINT) for recurring hint families.
- Item 3: Measurement + either README update or TOON serializer improvement if claim is wrong.
- Item 4: Design doc; no code change until the redundancy is confirmed.

Surfaced by RusticMeadow during /ux-audit sweep, 2026-04-23.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-23T21:24:05.956479Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T21:24:05.956479Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["hints","mcp","robot","ux"]}

 {"id":"ft-xkuw9","title":"retry: ft-gc4hz regression test references private telemetry fields (build broken)","description":"Commit 765743d5 shipped cancelled_error_does_not_trip_circuit_breaker_ft_gc4hz which reads circuit.telemetry().failures_recorded and .successes_recorded directly. These fields are private on CircuitBreakerTelemetry — only the snapshot() accessor is public. Result: frankenterm-core does not compile under --no-default-features (E0616 at retry.rs:880:44 and :881:44). Fix landed in this commit: switch to circuit.telemetry().snapshot() and read the Snapshot's public fields.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:15:31.502945Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:22:49.654446Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:22:49.653600Z","close_reason":"Landed in 79029eeb — telemetry().snapshot() replaces private-field access in the ft-gc4hz regression test. crate now compiles on retry.rs's behalf; ft-kqidb still blocks full build.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-xo3u4","title":"[review][MEDIUM] ft-djpi3 wa.events_annotate changed-bit derivation is TOCTOU-racy across concurrent annotate calls","description":"commit 0a865e6f derives wa.events_annotate's 'changed' field by READ-WRITE-READ-DIFF across four independent storage calls with NO surrounding transaction at crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs:4292-4368:

    let prior_annotations = storage.get_event_annotations(params.event_id).await?...;   // TXN 1 (read)
    let prior_note = prior_annotations.note.clone();
    storage.set_event_note(params.event_id, params.note.clone(), params.by.clone()).await?;   // TXN 2 (write)
    ... record audit ...                                                                 // TXN 3 (write)
    let annotations = storage.get_event_annotations(params.event_id).await?...;          // TXN 4 (read)
    let changed = prior_note != annotations.note;

Two concurrent wa.events_annotate calls (same event_id, different notes) race:

    Client A: TXN 1 reads prior=None
    Client B: TXN 1 reads prior=None    <-- same pre-state
    Client A: TXN 2 writes 'foo'
    Client B: TXN 2 writes 'bar'        <-- B overwrites A
    Client A: TXN 4 reads note='bar'    <-- A sees B's write, NOT its own
    Client A: changed = None != Some('bar') = TRUE   <-- A claims success despite being overwritten
    Client B: TXN 4 reads note='bar'
    Client B: changed = None != Some('bar') = TRUE

Client A's envelope reports changed=true AND annotations.note='bar' — but A wrote 'foo'. An agent reading the envelope would conclude 'my annotation is live' when it isn't. For multi-agent swarms that use wa.events_annotate to leave a handoff trace on events, this is silent data loss.

The first-event-no-prior case is handled correctly (changed=true for a first write that transitions None→Some; changed=false for clear-on-already-empty) — tests events_annotate_reports_changed_for_real_write_and_noop_rewrite and events_annotate_reports_changed_false_when_clearing_absent_note at :5276-5336 cover the serial case.

## Fix direction

Option A (smallest diff): wrap the read-write-read sequence in a single SQLite transaction so the prior/new reads are consistent with this call's write.

Option B: have set_event_note return (prior_note, new_note) atomically from within its own UPDATE statement (e.g., RETURNING clause + snapshot); compute changed from the return value, no second read needed.

Option C: make the 'changed' semantics 'the write landed and differed from THIS CALL's observation of prior state' — document that concurrent callers may both see changed=true. Weaker contract but simpler.

## Why MEDIUM, not HIGH

- wa.events_annotate concurrency pressure in current deployments is low (annotation is an operator action, not a high-rate path).
- Data loss is bounded (last-write-wins, standard SQLite semantics); the misleading 'changed' bit is reporting-level only.
- Not a ship-blocker; a Linear-FIXME-style follow-up.

Severity: MEDIUM. Audit round 15, 2026-04-23. Tag: review, mcp, concurrency, toctou.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T19:50:13.946117Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:59:47.119341Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:59:47.119025Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 9fcb4df4 (Option C from the bead). `changed` now derives from `prior_note != params.note` — the caller's own requested note vs the prior value observed by THIS call — instead of comparing against a non-transactional re-read that could already reflect a concurrent writer's overwrite. Stops the specific silent-data-loss pathology the bead cited: Client A will no longer emit `changed: true, annotations.note: <B's value>` as if A's write succeeded. annotations in the envelope still reflects authoritative on-disk state, so a client noticing `annotations.note != params.note` can explicitly detect the last-write-wins race. Option B (set_event_note returning prior atomically via a surrounding transaction) is left as a deeper follow-up — ripples into WriteCommand + the sync helper signature. Existing serial tests at mcp_tools.rs:5336-5398 continue to hold (None→Some=true, Some→Some=false, None→None=false all derive identically under the new rule).
